title: Software Engineering
description: Practice year-wise MCQs from 2018 to 2026 with explanations and answer review.

questions.prompt: Which model is known for sequential development phases?
questions.explanation: The waterfall model follows a linear sequence of phases such as requirement, design, implementation, testing, and maintenance.
Requirement engineering focuses on collecting and defining system requirements.

questions.prompt: Which testing method tests individual units of code?
questions.explanation: Unit testing checks the smallest testable parts of a software system.

questions.prompt: Coupling in software design refers to:
options: The degree of interdependence between modules
questions.answer: The degree of interdependence between modules
questions.explanation: Coupling measures how closely connected software modules are.
Agile methods focus on iterative development and continuous feedback.

options: Relatedness of elements in a module
questions.answer: Relatedness of elements in a module
questions.explanation: High cohesion means the elements inside a module are closely related.

questions.prompt: Which phase follows coding in the waterfall model?
questions.explanation: In the waterfall model, testing follows implementation.

questions.prompt: A software prototype is mainly used to:
options: Gather early feedback and clarify requirements
questions.answer: Gather early feedback and clarify requirements
questions.explanation: Prototyping helps stakeholders understand and refine requirements early.

options: Corrective, adaptive, perfective, and preventive work
questions.answer: Corrective, adaptive, perfective, and preventive work
questions.explanation: Maintenance covers a range of activities after deployment to keep software useful and reliable.
